chosen_message_reachability.note: no chosen message within budget drove a post-absorb coordinate to V_STAR; absorb mixes through T rounds so targeting a single coordinate value is preimage-hard. Not a proof of impossibility.

confirm_collapse.note: j_B(u, V_STAR)=0 for all u (confirmed); off the hyperplane the slope (1+GAMMA*v) is nonzero so j_B is injective in u.

constant_field_preimage.note: psi == c maps to V_STAR on ALL 256 cells (one-round full-lattice singular collapse). This zeroes the injection for ONE round; the accumulator still evolves via MU*Cd + A_C*Cd^2 + rho_t. Only this single constant state has full-lattice collapse (unique cubic root).

description: Singular-hyperplane (v=V_STAR) attack audit for Candidate B

equations.constant_preimage: F(c) = V_STAR with c = 357959172 (unique cubic root)
equations.injection: j_B(u, v) = u + GAMMA*u*v = u*(1 + GAMMA*v) mod p

limitations: chosen-message reachability is a bounded search (4000 messages)
constant-field preimage is the only exact full-lattice construction found; partial-lattice singular constructions (preimage-hard) were not exhausted
no proof that v_star is globally unreachable under the normative protocol

multi_round_attraction.note: over T rounds the wave field transits V_STAR at the uniform rate; no attractor toward the singular hyperplane.

path_erasure_test.note: perturbing the full-collapse constant state changes the digest every time: the single-round injection-zeroing does NOT erase the perturbed cell's contribution, because that cell still enters the accumulator at adjacent rounds (as u at round t, as v at round t-1) and through wave-field diffusion. No path erasure observed.

reachability_family.note: the Phase 8J family collapses the wave to terminal 0 (0 != V_STAR); no family state lands on the singular hyperplane.

reachability_random.note: V_STAR is hit at the uniform-random rate ~1/p per coordinate; no structural attraction observed in random states.

reduced_model_singular.note: j_B(u,v)=0 exactly on the union {u=0} U {v=v_star}; this is 2p-1 pairs out of p^2, i.e. fraction ~2/p -> 0. The singular set is a single hyperplane v=v_star (measure 1/p), same order as Candidate A's zero set. It does NOT concentrate collisions beyond measure ~1/p.

verdict.conclusion: The singular hyperplane v=V_STAR is constructible (unique constant state c=357959172 maps the whole lattice to V_STAR for one round) but NOT broadly reachable (random/eigenmode/family states hit it at rate ~1/p) and does NOT cause path erasure (perturbing a full-collapse state changes the digest every time). The singular set has measure ~1/p, the same order as Candidate A's injection-zero set. No practical trajectory-commitment collision family was produced from it.
verdict.singular_set_measure: ~1/p (single hyperplane), same order as Candidate A's zero set
